findings: Stuck alerts fire when a claimed task file is older than 120s, but claim mtimes do not advance during normal execution, so long tasks can false-positive as stuck.
Alert dedupe uses substring matching in ALERTED, so task IDs with overlapping substrings can suppress or misroute alerts.
Recovery is notification-only for stuck work; requeue only happens in orphan recovery when pane census shows no live agents and claim age exceeds 180s.
If tmux pane census is wrong, orphan recovery can requeue claimed work even while agents are still active, creating duplicate-work risk.
All-agents-dead partial completion can trigger after a short grace period if pane detection is wrong or total task counting is off.
